channel.title: Nature Materials
channel.description: Nature Materials is a multidisciplinary journal aimed at bringing together cutting-edge research across the entire spectrum of materials science and technology. Nature Materials covers all applied and fundamental aspects of the synthesis/processing, structure/composition, properties and performance of materials. Nature Materials provides a forum for the development of a common identity among materials scientists while encouraging researchers to cross established subdisciplinary lines. To achieve this, Nature Materials takes an interdisciplinary, integrated and balanced approach to all areas of materials research while fostering the exchange of ideas between scientists involved in different communities.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Scalable manufacture of nearly pure-phase metallic MoS<sub>2</sub> nanosheets
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 29 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02480-2

A microwave-assisted process is developed for the rapid and scalable manufacture of pure-phase metallic MoS2 nanosheets, enabling practical electrochemical devices for energy applications.
item.dc:title: Scalable manufacture of nearly pure-phase metallic MoS<sub>2</sub> nanosheets
item.dc:creator: Ziwei Jeffrey Yang
Zhuangnan Li
Leyi Loh
James Moloney
John Walmsley
Jiahang Li
Yuan Chen
Lixin Liu
Han Zang
Han Yan
Soumya Sarkar
Jason Day
Yan Wang
Manish Chhowalla

item.title: Water-soluble hexagonal BaAl<sub>2</sub>O<sub>4</sub> as sacrificial layer for freestanding crystalline membranes and flexible devices
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 29 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02486-w

Freestanding membranes of hexagonal oxides remain difficult to obtain. In this work, a water-soluble crystalline hexagonal BaAl2O4 is found to serve as a sacrificial layer for obtaining freestanding membranes with six-fold or three-fold symmetry.
item.dc:title: Water-soluble hexagonal BaAl<sub>2</sub>O<sub>4</sub> as sacrificial layer for freestanding crystalline membranes and flexible devices
item.dc:creator: Mengcheng Li

item.title: Collective photon emission and ferroelectric exciton ordering near Mott insulating state in WSe<sub>2</sub>/WS<sub>2</sub> heterobilayers
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 29 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-025-02476-4

Ferroelectric ordering of moiré excitons and collective photon emission emerge in WSe2/WS2 heterobilayers near the Mott state driven by strong dipolar interactions and a transition to symmetry-broken ferroelectric phases.
item.dc:title: Collective photon emission and ferroelectric exciton ordering near Mott insulating state in WSe<sub>2</sub>/WS<sub>2</sub> heterobilayers
item.dc:creator: Luka Matej Devenica
Zach Hadjri
Jan Kumlin

James Hone
Kenji Watanabe

Thomas Pohl
Ajit Srivastava

item.title: Exploiting and taming the structural instability for computer memories
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 27 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02481-1

A phase-change material enables in-memory computing by well-controlled multilevel switching without resistance drift.
item.dc:title: Exploiting and taming the structural instability for computer memories
item.dc:creator: A. Lindsay Greer

item.title: Intertwined orders in a quantum-entangled metal
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 27 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-025-02475-5

Resonant inelastic X-ray scattering interferometry reveals a highly entangled electronic phase in Nd2Ir2O7, enabling extraction of its entanglement structure and confirming the cubic-symmetry-breaking order predicted from complementary Raman spectroscopy.
item.dc:title: Intertwined orders in a quantum-entangled metal
item.dc:creator: Junyoung Kwon
Jaehwon Kim
Gwansuk Oh

Beom Hyun Kim
B. J. Kim

item.title: Narrowband quantum emitters in hexagonal boron nitride with optically addressable spins
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 27 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-025-02458-6

Producing isolated single-photon emitters in hexagonal boron nitride with predefined spin transitions is challenging. Oxygen annealing enables the controlled fabrication of narrowband quantum emitters with optically active spin for quantum applications.
item.dc:title: Narrowband quantum emitters in hexagonal boron nitride with optically addressable spins
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Whitefield
Helen Zhi Jie Zeng
James Liddle-Wesolowski
Islay O. Robertson
Ádám Ganyecz
